response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the résumé spans roughly 2006 to present (~19-20 years), clearly meeting the 15-year minimum, and includes software, embedded, and some hardware-adjacent work. Step 2: Education — Bachelor's in Computer Science satisfies the minimum, but there is no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field. Step 3: Seniority — titles top out at Staff/Senior Software Engineer; the role is a director-level IC expected to shape AI strategy, advise strategic customers, and inspire organizations. There is no evidence of that scope: no principal/director roles, no strategy or advisory work, no large-org influence. Step 4: AI expertise — recent work at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which is relevant but recent and tooling-focused; there is no recognized AI expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms at scale, no publications or public AI thought leadership. Step 5: Entrepreneurial signal — founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and workshop/conference speaking show initiative and knowledge-sharing, matching the 'inspire others' and startup-experience preferences partially. Step 6: Cloud — hands-on AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker experience exists, but it is implementation-level rather than cloud product strategy. Overall: solid, versatile senior/staff engineer who meets the minimums but is one or two career levels below the role's leadership and recognized-AI-expertise expectations.
strengths: ~19 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience aligned with the AI focus area
Entrepreneurial track record (GISCollective founder) plus workshops and conference talks showing knowledge-sharing
concerns: No principal/director-level roles or evidence of organization-wide technical strategy and influence
No recognized AI expertise, advanced AI degree, or demonstrated AI products/platforms at scale
Cloud experience is implementation-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), not cloud product strategy or customer advisory

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs director-level IC scope
Limited recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Meets 15-year experience and degree minimums with broad engineering depth

response.justification: The candidate comfortably clears the minimum qualifications and brings broad, credible engineering depth with recent AI-agent tooling work and entrepreneurial initiative. However, this role is a director-level strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products, and experience advising executives and strategic customers — dimensions the résumé does not evidence. He profiles as a strong staff-level engineer rather than a principal-level AI strategist, so he is a stretch fit for this particular role.
